reviewed_items.reviewer_notes: An attacker can trigger this via P2P DataColumnSidecarsByRange RPC by requesting a large range that forces quota exhaustion mid-block. The quota check at L164-165 exits immediately when quota reaches zero, breaking atomicity - if a block has sidecars [1,2,3] and quota=2, only sidecars 1-2 are sent, violating the spec requirement that all sidecars of a block must be included if any sidecar is included. This causes reconstruction failure for peers relying on complete sidecar sets for PeerDAS data availability sampling. Severity remains Medium as this affects Prysm's 31% network share (>5% threshold) through degraded data availability but does not directly cause node crashes or validator slashing.
